free_text: on keV capture of the structural materials Cr,Fe,Ni,
Karlsruhe, 8-9 May, 1973, summary of KFK capture
and transmission results on Cr-50 and Fe-54.
MACS for Cr,Fe,Ni,Co isotopes in Table.
with neutron and radiation widths for Cr-52, Cr-53,
Fe-54, Fe-57, Ni-62 and Ni-64. Point capture data.
transmission results, Cr-50, Cr-52, Ni-62, Ni-64.
keV neutron radiative capture and total cross
Pulsed 3 MV Van de Graaff at Karlsruhe.
In the capture measurement, the sample to be analyzed
was interchanged with a carbon sample and a gold sample
for background and neutron flux determination,
respectively. The sample material was pressed into
flat cylindrical bronze containers with a diameter of
8 cm. Because of the small amount of 64Ni available, a
bronze container of 4.5 cm diameter had to be used.
The gold sample was a disc of 9 cm dia. x 0.1 cm
thickness. The carbon samples were graphite discs
chosen to match the scattering cross section of the
areas (narrow levels) extracted with area analysis
least-squares fit to transmission data, R-matrix
formalism. Inelastic channel taken into account for
time-  dependent background(measured with C sample),
sample admixtures (O in oxides, isotopic impurities)
unrecorded pulse height spectrum fraction below
intrinsic tank efficiency (escape of entire cascade)
.Uncertainty in energy scale and resonance energies
from transmission and capture yield uncertainties
by error propagation through least-squares fit.
.Capture cross section data are capture yields divided
by sample thickness in at./b, equal to true cross
section only where effects of sample thickness (self-
shielding, multiple collisions) and resolution are

the neutron width are given in Table 18 of
